evidence.scientific_clarity: Forest plot shows all 12 studies with CI lines and inverse-variance weighted squares, diamond for pooled FE estimate, dashed null line at 0 — fully matches brief.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype; PDF uses Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans (default Matplotlib font), not editable Type 42/TrueType text.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1a6faf) for study squares, dark red (#c0392b) for pooled diamond — colorblind-distinguishable; no misuse of green/red for direction.
evidence.layout_density: Study labels left, numeric effect+CI column right, separator line between studies and pooled, column headers present — panel space well used with no gaps or overlaps.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes squares, CI lines, diamond, null line, and gives exact pooled estimate (0.245, 95% CI [0.196, 0.294]); legend is not needed as labels are inline.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is a real PDF (vector paths), but fonts are embedded as Type 3 (not Type 42), so text is not selectable/editable in Illustrator; script lacks pdf.fonttype=42.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the measure (log OR), describes all visual elements (squares with area ∝ weight, CI lines, red diamond), gives specific numerical finding with 95% CI, and names the method (inverse-variance fixed-effect).

overall_strengths: Scientifically complete forest plot: all 12 studies, properly weighted squares, CI lines, pooled diamond, and null-effect reference line.
Caption is thorough and publication-ready, including exact numerical pooled estimate, CI, and methodological details.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and rcParams equivalent) — PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type 3 bitmapped glyphs, making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector editors.
Default DejaVu Sans font is used throughout instead of a publication-quality serif or sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a LaTeX-compatible font).
